compiled_state.title: Brainstorm confirmation questions have ambiguous yes/no semantics
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description: When brainstorm asks confirmation questions like 'Does this capture it correctly, or is anything missing?', answering 'yes' is ambiguous — it could mean 'yes it captures it correctly' or 'yes something is missing'. Confirmation prompts that request a clear yes/no answer should have unambiguous meaning. Either use single-clause questions ('Does this capture it correctly?') or use explicit labeled options instead of yes/no.

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral (dual-signal: ticket describes brainstorm prompt behavior + affected file is skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md)
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Gate 1a: intent-aligned (high confidence). Proceeding to investigation.
